finding: ATM-GOV-0193/0194 added gitignored runtime telemetry, but raw/high-frequency build statistics are still duplicated into tracked runner-sync receipts. The repository currently tracks 18 `*.runner-sync-receipt.json` files; recent receipts include full incremental plans, phase timings, cache details, and runtime telemetry references rather than a minimal decision digest.
expectedBehavior: All raw statistics and logs should be written to local disk under .atm/runtime/telemetry or another gitignored log surface. This includes counters, per-run timing events, validation usage, debug logs, broker decision traces, runner-sync receipts, and other high-frequency telemetry. Git history should keep only compact decision digests, close summaries, or deliberately selected baselines; repeated raw telemetry/log/receipt streams must not be committed by default.
evidenceOrRepro: `git ls-files '.atm/history/evidence/*runner-sync-receipt.json'` returns 18 tracked receipts. ATM-GOV-0194 and ATM-GOV-0195 receipts are 2674 and 3524 bytes and contain `incrementalPlan`, `phaseTimingsMs`, `tsBuildCache`, and `treatmentTelemetry`, while the same per-run data already exists under `.atm/runtime/telemetry/runner-sync-build/**`.
followUp: Split runner-sync output into a local detailed receipt and a tracked compact decision digest. The tracked form should retain task/source/output/config digests, decision class, rollback reference, and local-runtime pointer only; per-phase timing series, changed-path lists, cache internals, and repeated counters stay gitignored.
